content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit for Freelancers: Solve Collaboration Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project bottlenecks. Use this advanced SaaS starter kit checklist to master collaboration using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Collaboration bottlenecks kill freelance margins. By deploying a standardized SaaS starter kit, you unify your architecture, automate boilerplate, and enable seamless multi-agent workflows. Using Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you can transform chaotic project handoffs into a professional, high-velocity development engine.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: a client project starts with high hopes, but quickly devolves into a fragmented mess of conflicting branches, undocumented logic, and 'who wrote this?' moments. Collaboration is the hidden tax on every freelancer, bleeding your time and eroding your reputation.

It doesn't have to be this way. The secret to scaling isn't working harder; it's building a deterministic foundation. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you create a source of truth that turns your development process into a repeatable, high-fidelity system that clients respect and competitors fear.

steps.title: Standardize the Repository Architecture
steps.description: Establish a rigid directory structure to ensure that any developer (or AI) knows exactly where logic, styles, and data schemas reside.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a robust SaaS starter repository structure using Next.js 15, TypeScript, and Tailwind, ensuring strict separation of concerns between API routes and UI components.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure with strict directory naming conventions and base TSConfig.'

steps.title: Automate Type-Safe API Contracts
steps.description: Eliminate communication gaps between frontend and backend by enforcing strict schema synchronization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Zod schemas for all API inputs and outputs, and generate TRPC routers to ensure type safety across the entire stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate TRPC router and Zod schemas based on the existing user management model.'

steps.title: Configure Multi-Agent Development Environment
steps.description: Prepare your codebase for AI-assisted collaboration by adding context-aware documentation and prompt manifests.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an .ai-instructions file that defines project coding standards, styling conventions, and architecture decisions for future AI agents.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate comprehensive project documentation in .ai-instructions to guide agentic workflows.'

steps.title: Implement Automated Quality Gates
steps.description: Ensure code quality remains high during collaborative cycles by setting up pre-commit hooks that enforce linting and testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Husky and lint-staged to run ESLint and Vitest on every commit, rejecting any code that violates project standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Setup Husky git hooks for pre-commit linting and testing enforcement.'

steps.title: Synchronize State and Deployment Pipelines
steps.description: Connect your CI/CD pipeline to a staging environment so clients can review progress without manual hand-offs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a GitHub Actions workflow that automates deployment to Vercel on push to the 'main' branch, including automated smoke tests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create github-actions workflow for automated deployment and testing.'

pro_tips: Treat your starter kit as a product, not a template; version it on GitHub and pull updates into new projects regularly.
Use 'context injection' by keeping a summarized 'Project Bible' in the root directory for Claude Code to read before every task.
Automate your database migrations using Drizzle or Prisma and include them in the starter kit to prevent schema drift across environments.
Always set up environment variable validation at runtime to fail fast if a client provides incorrect configurations.
Leverage ephemeral preview environments for every PR to allow non-technical clients to see changes without touching your local machine.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, create a 'Client Onboarding' document that explains how they can use the preview links to provide feedback, effectively closing the loop on collaboration.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your own tooling and start shipping high-value features for your clients with a system that works for you. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
